question_text: All electromagnetic waves travel at the same speed in a vacuum. What is this speed?

options.label: D
options.text: It depends on the type of EM wave

explanation: The speed of light (and all EM waves) in a vacuum is 3 × 10⁸ m/s, or 300,000 km/s. This is always the same, whether it's radio waves, visible light, or X-rays.
